mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains at or near cyclical lows relative to its trailing 12-month average signals accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following low-rate readings are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over the trailing 3-month period
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE rose >0.3pp over 3 months, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the signal's rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across two independent decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly indicate that monetary tightening is transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive demand, amplifying recession risk beyond what either series predicts alone and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% YoY while FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing downturn
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-decline/FEDFUNDS-elevated signal fails to produce negative median 60-day forward returns relative to the unconditional distribution, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power over the additive single-series baseline (incremental R^2 < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when payroll employment (PAYEMS) is simultaneously expanding, indicating the economy is in a mid-cycle expansion where production gains translate into sustained earnings growth rather than late-cycle overheating.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0 and PAYEMS 3-month average change > 0
failure_condition: During periods classified as mid-cycle expansion by the trigger condition, 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO readings are not significantly different from returns in non-expansion periods (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in a majority of expansion sub-periods tested.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending, the dominant driver of GDP, and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in stronger near-term corporate revenue.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 across rolling 2-year windows, or the sign of the relationship flips in more than one out of three tested sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal a labor-market turning point that is a stronger negative predictor of forward equity returns than either series individually, because the combination rules out noisy single-month payroll revisions as the driver.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth decelerates by more than 30% relative to the prior 6-month average while UNRATE rises
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cycle peak
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration/UNRATE-rise signal underperforms a simple UNRATE-only threshold model out-of-sample (lower rank IC or lower hit rate) in more than half of tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above a threshold consistent with the Fed's implicit comfort zone, markets price in a higher probability of future tightening or delayed easing, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following CPI YoY readings above 3.0% are not significantly lower than baseline unconditional returns (p > 0.05), or the effect is absent in more than half of historical episodes with comparable inflation levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns specifically when it occurs in a late-cycle regime characterized by an already-elevated federal funds rate, because housing is the most interest-rate-sensitive component of demand and its contraction foreshadows a broader consumption slowdown that the market has not yet fully priced.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated policy rate
failure_condition: During periods classified as late-cycle with elevated FEDFUNDS, 5-day forward returns following HOUST declines are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the relationship is only observable in a single historical episode rather than recurring across cycles.
